title: Sheinbaum Announces National Water Law Ending Commercialization, Cracking Down on Overexploitation
detail: At her Monday mañanera, President Sheinbaum detailed the new National Water Law, which ends water commercialization by private companies, requires overexploiters to pay approximately 12 billion pesos in accumulated debts, and punishes water hoarding with fines up to 3.5 million pesos or imprisonment. She also reaffirmed that a recall referendum (revocación de mandato) would take place in 2027 as a constitutional democratic mechanism. Sheinbaum signed a decree officially recognizing community tourism for historically marginalized Indigenous communities. She also announced the FGR is actively investigating funeral homes 'Rueda' and 'El Ángel de Iguala' for potential links to the enforced disappearance of the 43 Ayotzinapa students.

title: Mexico Dispatches Humanitarian Aid Ship to Cuba, Considers Oil Shipments for Energy Crisis
detail: Mexico sent a humanitarian aid ship to Cuba to help address the island's acute energy crisis, which has produced widespread blackouts. Sheinbaum announced Mexico was also studying the feasibility of oil shipments to Cuba, reaffirming solidarity with Havana in the face of continued U.S. sanctions pressure. The assistance comes as the U.S. Trump administration intensified pressure on regional governments to sever ties with Cuba. Two of three aid vessels later lost radio contact, triggering a Navy search operation announced on March 27.
